---
title: Microstructure-Aware Reinforcement Learning for High-Frequency Trading
author: Krzysztof Wojdalski
date: now
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY HH:mm [UTC]"
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: pracamgrwne
    latex-engine: xelatex
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
    output-dir: ../../build
    linestretch: 1.5
    header-includes:
      - \usepackage[utf8]{inputenc}
      - \usepackage{amsthm}
      - \usepackage{rotating}
      - \usepackage{algorithm}
      - \usepackage{algpseudocode}
      - \usepackage{amsmath}
      - \usepackage{booktabs}
      - \usepackage{longtable}
      - \listoffigures
      - \listoftables
      - \nralbumu{310284}
      - \speciality{Economics}
      - \opiekun{dr Pawel Sakowski}
      - \keywords{Reinforcement Learning, High-Frequency Trading, Order Book, Single-Asset Trading, TD3}
      - \dziedzina{14.3 Economics}
      - \klasyfikacja{G11, G17, C63}
      - \tytulang{Microstructure-Aware Reinforcement Learning for High-Frequency Trading}
  docx:
    embed-resources: true
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    number-sections: true
    number-depth: 2
    toc-location: right
    format-links: false
    other-links:
      - text: PDF
        href: /masters_thesis/masters-thesis.pdf
        icon: file-pdf
bibliography: ../../bibliography/library.bib
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
  latex_engine: xelatex
jupyter:
  kernelspec:
    display_name: Python 3
    language: python
    name: python3
thesis_runtime_config:
  scenario: "src/configs/scenarios/aapl_td3_hft_lob_state_space.yaml"
  feature_config_primary: "src/configs/features/hft_lob_features_state_space.yaml"
  feature_config_baseline: "src/configs/features/hft_lob_features.yaml"
  experiment_name: "TRADINGENV_TD3_AAPL_HFT_STATE_SPACE"
---

\theoremstyle{definition}
\newtheorem{definition}{Definition}
\newpage

\begin{abstract}

This thesis studies whether a deep reinforcement learning agent using
order-book-derived microstructure features can produce robust, risk-adjusted
trading performance in a high-frequency single-asset setting. The work focuses
on continuous portfolio exposure control and addresses the practical constraints
— execution modeling, transaction cost sensitivity, and evaluation discipline —
that most often invalidate paper-trading results.

A TD3 agent is trained on high-frequency AAPL limit order book data and
evaluated against benchmark strategies under explicit simulation assumptions.
The agent achieves positive out-of-sample risk-adjusted returns that exceed the
evaluated benchmarks, and a microstructure-aware state representation
constructed from order flow, spread regime, and book shape features demonstrates
measurable performance improvement over a simpler snapshot-based baseline.
Statistical validation and transaction-cost sensitivity analysis are used to
separate genuine policy quality from favorable training stochasticity and to
bound the conditions under which the observed edge persists.

The main contribution is an end-to-end, reproducible pipeline linking LOB
feature engineering, Differential Sharpe Ratio reward design, TD3 training, and
rigorous out-of-sample evaluation — together with an explicit account of the
simulation assumptions that separate these findings from deployable performance
claims.

\end{abstract}

\pagebreak


{{< include ./01-00-introduction.qmd >}}

{{< include ./01-01-scope-and-objectives.qmd >}}
{{< include ./01-02-thesis-organization.qmd >}}

\newpage
{{< include ./02-00-literature-review.qmd >}}
{{< include ./02-01-tick-level-order-book-trading.qmd >}}
{{< include ./02-02-market-microstructure-mechanisms.qmd >}}
{{< include ./02-03-from-signals-to-decisions.qmd >}}
{{< include ./02-04-competing-modeling-approaches.qmd >}}
{{< include ./02-05-reinforcement-learning-for-trading.qmd >}}
{{< include ./02-06-trading-problem-formulation.qmd >}}
{{< include ./02-07-applied-rl-trading-evidence.qmd >}}
{{< include ./02-08-implications-for-this-thesis.qmd >}}

\newpage
{{< include ./03-00-reinforcement-learning.qmd >}}
{{< include ./03-01-rl-categories.qmd >}}
{{< include ./03-02-actor-critic-methods.qmd >}}

\newpage
{{< include ./04-00-design-of-the-trading-agent.qmd >}}
{{< include ./04-01-action-space.qmd >}}
{{< include ./04-02-state-space.qmd >}}
{{< include ./04-03-reward-function.qmd >}}
{{< include ./04-04-value-function.qmd >}}
{{< include ./04-05-policy.qmd >}}
{{< include ./04-06-discount-factor.qmd >}}
{{< include ./04-07-optimization-hyperparameters.qmd >}}

\newpage
{{< include ./05-00-implementation.qmd >}}
{{< include ./05-01-data-preparation.qmd >}}
{{< include ./05-02-code.qmd >}}

\newpage
{{< include ./06-00-results.qmd >}}
{{< include ./06-01-statistical-validation.qmd >}}
{{< include ./06-02-robustness-assessment.qmd >}}
{{< include ./06-03-performance-evaluation.qmd >}}

\newpage
{{< include ./07-00-conclusions.qmd >}}

{{< include ./98-bibliography.qmd >}}
{{< include ./99-appendix.qmd >}}
{{< include ./99-glossary.qmd >}}
